method: Full-corpus Sharpe comparison between DOMEX-session lessons and non-DOMEX-session lessons. DOMEX sessions identified from SWARM-LANES.md + SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md. Sharpe extracted from lesson headers.
expect: Sharpe premium for DOMEX lanes (8.2 vs 7.8) will NOT replicate at n>300: predict premium <0.2 at larger sample
actual: DOMEX mean=7.94 (n=540), non-DOMEX mean=6.94 (n=17). Premium=+1.001, Cohen d=0.774. BUT: 97% of Sharpe-rated lessons are DOMEX (540/557). Non-DOMEX n=17 is too small for reliable comparison. The S489 finding (8.2 vs 7.8, n=555 total) used a different methodology — mine finds a LARGER premium but with a near-empty comparison group. The premium is REAL but UNTESTABLE: DOMEX became the norm so thoroughly that non-DOMEX Sharpe-rated lessons barely exist.
diff: Predicted premium <0.2 (noise). Got +1.001 (large effect). But falsification target was wrong: the question is not whether premium exists, but whether it is TESTABLE. With 97% DOMEX prevalence, the causal claim (DOMEX causes quality) is unfalsifiable — confounded with era, Sharpe-assignment norms, and survival bias. The REAL finding: DOMEX adoption was so complete that the control group effectively disappeared, making the premium claim UNFALSIFIABLE, not false.
confounds: Era effect: Sharpe ratings started being consistently applied during DOMEX era
Selection bias: 97% of Sharpe-rated lessons are DOMEX (control group too small)
Survival bias: non-DOMEX lessons with low Sharpe may have been compacted away
Methodological inconsistency: S489 (n=555) and this measurement (n=557) used different DOMEX classification methods
verdict: FALSIFICATION INCONCLUSIVE — premium appears real (+1.0) but comparison is UNFALSIFIABLE due to control group collapse (n=17). DOMEX adoption was so successful that the counterfactual no longer exists. This is a Lakatos-style degenerative confirmation: the hypothesis cannot be tested because the intervention became universal.
